memoryArtifacts.headline: Hucbald, Raoul de Gouy and Gauthier de Valois in Picardie, Cambrésis, Ile-de-France & Champagne, pg. 6
memoryArtifacts.description:
Hucbald, Raoul de Gouy and Gauthier de Valois in Picardie, Cambrésis, Ile-de-France & Champagne, pg. 6
~http://racineshistoire.free.fr/LGN/PDF/Vermandois-Valois-Vexin.pdf
However, it must be noted that this racineshistorie pedigree contains a number of discrepancies disputed by many other historical documents, such as making Raoul de Cambrai (who died in 944) the father of Raoul de Gouy (who died in 926). The reverse is actually correct: Raoul de Gouy is the father of Raoul de Cambrai. A second discrepancy is making Raoul de Gouy's wife Hildegard, when most historical records name Raoul de Gouy's wife Adelais, the daughter of French King Charles III (Charles the Simple) and sister of King Louis IV.

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
geni.com
Adélaïde d'Anjou, Reine de France
Also Known As:	"Blanche", "the White", "Adelaide "the White" d'Anjou", "Adela /d'Anjou/", "Blanca /d'Anjou/", "Adelaide /de Anjou/", "Blanche /de Anjou/", "Blanche /De Anjou/", "Adelais", "/Blanche/", "Arsinde d'Anjou", "Adelaide the White", "Blanche d'Anjou", "Blanca", "Arside Blanche d'Anjou"
Birthdate:	circa 946
Birthplace:	Anjou, now, Pays de la Loire, France
Death:	May 29, 1026 (75-84)
Arles, Bouches-du-Rhone, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France
Place of Burial:	Arles, Bouches-du-Rhone, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France
Immediate Family:	
Daughter of Fulk II, Count of Anjou and Gerberge du Gâtinais, comtesse d'Anjou

Wife of 
Étienne II de Brioude, comte de Gévaudan; 
Raymond III, count of Toulouse & Prince of Gothia; 
Louis V 'le Fainéant’ de France, Roi de Francie Occidentale and 
Guillaume II "le Liberateur" comte de Provence

Mother of 
Ermengarde de Toulouse; 
Pons, comte de Gévaudan; 
Ermengarde / Humberge de Limoges de Gévaudan, [daughter of Etienne II de Gévaudan and 
ADelais d'Anjou; 
Almodis de Limoges de Gévaudan;
 Eimilde de Gévaudan; 
Guillaume III Taillefer, comte de Toulouse; 
Tota de Toulouse; 
Constance d'Arles, queen consort of the Franks; 
Lucia de Provence, comtesse consort de Razès; 
Guillaume III le Pieux, comte de Provence and 
Ermengarde of Arles « less
Sister of Agnes d'Anjou; Guy d'Anjou; 
Bouchard IV, dit "le Vénérable", III. comte de Vendôme; 
Geoffrey I "Greymantle", Count of Anjou; 
Adela d'Anjou; seigneur Foulques Ier de Briollay; 
Agnès d'Anjou; Aelips d'Anjou; 
'Countess Toulouse' d' Anjou Adelaise Arsinde Blanche; 
Guy d'Anjou, Bishop of Le Puy and 
Humbert d'Anjou 


Occupation:	Countess d'Anjou, Countess of Provence, Condesa de Reims, Grevinne, Co-Monarch of Aquitaine, Viscountess of Gévaudan, Countess of Toulouse, Queen of Aquitaine and of the West Franks, Countess and Margravine de Provence, Countess de Burgundy


Adelaide-Blanche of Anjou (c.? 940 - 1026) was the countess consort by marriage of Gévaudan and Forez, of Toulouse, of Provence, and of Burgundy; and queen consort of Aquitaine. She was the regent of Gevaudan during the minority of her sons in the 960s, and the regent of Provence during the minority of her stepson from 994 until 999.

«b»Life«/b»
She was the daughter of Fulk II, Count of Anjou, and Gerberga and sister of Geoffrey Greymantle. She successfully increased Angevin fortunes, being married a total of five times. Her family had become upwardly mobile to the point that, as a member of just the third generation from Ingelger, Adelaide-Blanche had married into the highest ranks of the older nobility of western Francia.

Her first marriage was to Stephen, the powerful Count of Gévaudan and Forez in eastern Aquitaine. She was no more than fifteen at the time and he was much older. Still, they had three children who survived to adulthood. Stephen died in the early 960s and after his death she ruled the lands as regent for her sons William, Pons and Bertrand. She continued to govern Gevaudan and Forez while her remaining two sons learned to rule their father's counties. Additionally, after her oldest son William's death in 975 she raised his infant son Stephen. Her brother Guy (a.k.a. Guido II) was made Count-Bishop of le Puy in 975 amidst local opposition and at his request Adelaide, acting for her sons Guy and Bertrand, led an army to aid him in establishing the "Peace of God" in le Puy.

In 982, as the widow of her second husband, Raymond, count of Toulouse, she wed Louis, son of King Lothair of France. The two were crowned King and Queen of Aquitaine at Brioude by her brother Bishop Guy of le Puy. The marriage lasted just over a year due to the couple being unable to peacefully live together. There was also a significant age difference - he being fifteen and Adelaide-Blanche being over forty. Adelaide found herself in a precarious situation with King Lothair but was rescued by Count William I of Provence whom she subsequently married in c.?984. Count William of Provence died in 994 shortly after becoming a monk at Avignon.

In 1010 king Robert II of France along with Odo II, Count of Blois went to Rome to secure an annulment from Robert's second wife, Constance of Arles, Adelaide-Blanche's daughter by William I. Pope Sergius IV, a friend to the Angevin counts, upheld the marriage and additionally upheld Adelaide's struggle to maintain control of lands at Montmajour Abbey. These lands, at Perth, had been donated by Count William I of Provence with his wife Adelaide-Blanche, as well as by a previous donation by William's father, Boson. A dispute over these lands arose by four brothers, sons of Nevolongus, who pope Sergius threatened with excommunication if they did not withdraw their claim. The claim was withdrawn and the lands remained under the control of Adelaide-Blanche acting as regent for her son William II of Provence.

It has been suggested that she married a fifth time, to Otto-William, Count of Burgundy, whose second wife was named Adelaide. However, it is disputed whether his wife Adelaide was the same as Adelaide-Blanche.

Adelaide-Blanche died in 1026, aged approximately eighty - six. The location of her death was probably at Avignon, since the year of her death is recorded by Arnoux, a monk of the abbey of Saint-André, near Avignon. She was buried in Montmajour Abbey, near Arles, considered at the time as the burial place of the family of counts of Provence.

«b»Marriages and children«/b»
She married first, c.? 955, Stephen, Count of Gévaudan. Children of this marriage were:

1.) William, (c.? 955 - 975).

2.) Pons, Count of Gévaudan and Forez. He died aft. 26 February 1011.

3.) Bertrand, Count of Gévaudan.

4.) Almodis of Gévaudan, she married Adalbert I de Charroux, Count de la Haute March.

Her second marriage was to Raymond III, Count of Toulouse and Prince of Gothia, in 975. He died in 978. She had by him at least one child:

1.)  William III, Count of Toulouse

She married, as her third husband, Louis V of France. The two were crowned King and Queen of Aquitaine, but the marriage ended in annulment.

As her fourth husband she married, c.?984, William I of Provence Together they had:

1.) William II of Provence

2.) Constance of Arles, who later married Robert II of France.

3.) Ermengarde, she married Robert I, Count of Auvergne.

4.) Tota-Adelaide, she married Bernard I, Count of Besalú.
__________________________________________________
Our royal, titled, noble and commoner ancestors
Adelheid of Anjou1,2
Last Edited	4 Apr 2020
F, #27027, d. 1026
Father	Fulk II 'the Good', Count of Anjou2 b. c 920, d. 11 Nov 958
Mother	Gerberge of the Gatinais d. 952
Charts	Some Descendants of Charlemagne
     
Adelheid of Anjou married Stephen, Count Gevaudan, son of Ermengaud, Count of Rouerge and Adelaide.3 

Adelheid of Anjou married Louis V, King of France, son of Lothair, King of France and Emma of Italy, in 982. Adelheid of Anjou and Louis V, King of France were divorced in 984.3 

Adelheid of Anjou married Guillaume II, Comte de Provence & Arles, son of Boson II, Count of Avignon & Arles and Constance of Vienne, between 984 and 986.2 

Adelheid of Anjou married Otto-William, Count of Bourgogne, Macon, Dijon, son of Adalbert, King of the Lombards and Gerberga of Macon, before 1016.3 Adelheid of Anjou died in 1026.3

zh.lifeSummary: 當Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou於0947年12月17日出生在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易麥-吉洛易安格斯時，她的父親Foulques II d'Anjou38歲，母親Gerberge de Gâtinais34歲。她於大約0975年與Raymond Count of Toulouse結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1026年5月29日在法國普洛福-阿爾培-o特 達阿爾布-度-o阿爾斯享年78歲，埋葬在法國普洛福-阿爾培-o特 達阿爾布-度-o阿爾斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou于0947年12月17日出生在法国帕斯 德 拉 洛易麥-吉洛易安格斯时，她的父亲Foulques II d'Anjou38岁，母亲Gerberge de Gâtinais34岁。她于大约0975年与Raymond Count of Toulouse结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1026年5月29日在法国普洛福-阿爾培-o特 達阿爾布-度-o阿爾斯享年78岁，埋葬在法国普洛福-阿爾培-o特 達阿爾布-度-o阿爾斯。

nameMeanings.name: Adélaïde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic origin (via French Adélaïde ), from adal ‘noble’ + heid ‘kind, sort’. It was borne in the 10th century by the wife of the Holy Roman Emperor Otto the Great. She became regent after his death and was revered as a saint. The given name increased in popularity in England during the 19th century, when it was borne by the wife of King William IV; she was the daughter of the ruler of the German duchy of Saxe-Meiningen. The Australian city of Adelaide was named in her honour.

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Blanche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a nickname for a blonde, from blanche, feminine of Old French blanc ‘white’ (of Germanic origin). It came to be associated with the notion of whiteness as indicating purity, and was introduced into England as a given name by the Normans. A pale complexion combined with light hair was long an ideal of beauty in Europe (compare Modern English fair, which at first meant ‘beautiful’ and then, from the 16th century, ‘light in colouring’).

nameMeanings.name: d'Anjou
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’, for someone from Anjou, a former province of western France (see Langevin ).

variants.name: Langevin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Normand, Andre, Henri, Jean-Louis, Pierre, Adelard, Emile, Gaston, Leodore, Lucien, Marcel.

French: habitational name, with fused definite article l', for an Angevin, i.e. for someone from Anjou, a former province of western France that was ruled by a count as an independent territory from the 10th century. The province became a property of the English Crown for fifty years at the end of the 12th century. Compare Longway and Lonsway .

History: Mathurin Langevin dit Lacroix from Le Lude in Sarthe, France, married Marie-Thérèse Martin in Quebec City, QC, in 1674. Michel Langevin from Paris, France, married Marguerite Guertin in Contrecoeur, QC, in 1714. Jean Bergevin dit Langevin from Angers in Maine-et-Loire, France, married Marie Piton in Quebec City, QC, in 1668. Charles Citoleux/Sitoleux dit Langevin from Chambellay in Maine-et-Loire, France, married Marie-Louise Harel in Montreal, QC, in 1739.

en.lifeSummary: When Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou was born on 17 December 0947, in Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France, her father, Foulques II d'Anjou, was 38 and her mother, Gerberge de Gâtinais, was 34. She married Raymond Count of Toulouse about 0975. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 29 May 1026, in Arles, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France, at the age of 78, and was buried in Arles, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou est née le 17 décembre 0947, à Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France, son père, Foulques II d'Anjou, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Gerberge de Gâtinais, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Raymond Count of Toulouse environ 0975. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 29 mai 1026, à Arles, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterrée à Arles, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou am 17. Dezember 0947 in Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Foulques II d'Anjou 38 und ihre Mutter Gerberge de Gâtinais 34. Sie hat um 0975 Raymond Count of Toulouse geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 29. Mai 1026 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Arles, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, Frankreich und wurde in Arles, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, Frankreich begraben.

it.Angers,49007,Maine-et-Loire,Pays de la L: Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia
Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia
it.lifeSummary: Quando Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou è nata il il 17 dicembre 0947 nel Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia, sua padre, Foulques II d'Anjou, aveva 38 anni e sua madre, Gerberge de Gâtinais, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato Raymond Count of Toulouse circa 0975. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 29 maggio 1026, nel Arles, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, Francia, all'età di 78 anni, e fu sepolta a Arles, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjouが0947年12月17日にフランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーメインエ-エト-ロイアーアンジャーズで生まれた時，父Foulques II d'Anjouは38歳，母Gerberge de Gâtinaisは34歳であった。約0975年にRaymond Count of Toulouseと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1026年5月29日にフランスプローベンセ-アルペス-クoト ド'アズルボウチェス-ドウ-ルhoンアルレスで78歳で死去し，フランスプローベンセ-アルペス-クoト ド'アズルボウチェス-ドウ-ルhoンアルレスにて埋葬された。

ko.Angers,49007,Maine-et-Loire,Pays de la L: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 앤그르스
프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 앤그르스
ko.lifeSummary: Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou(이)가 0947년 12월 17일 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 앤그르스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Foulques II d'Anjou(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Gerberge de Gâtinais(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 대략 0975년 Raymond Count of Toulouse(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1026년 5월 29일 프랑스 프로븐스-알페스-co트 드'아저 부체스-드-ho니 알레스에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 프로븐스-알페스-co트 드'아저 부체스-드-ho니 알레스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou nasceu em 17 de dezembro de 0947, em Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, França, seu pai, Foulques II d'Anjou, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Gerberge de Gâtinais, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com Raymond Count of Toulouse aproximadamente 0975. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 29 de maio de 1026, em Arles, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, França, com 78 anos, e foi sepultada em Arles, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, França.

ru.Angers,49007,Maine-et-Loire,Pays de la L: ангирс, меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция
ангирс, меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou родилась 17 декабря 0947 г. в местности ангирс, меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция, её отцу по имени Foulques II d'Anjou было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Gerberge de Gâtinais, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Raymond Count of Toulouse около 0975 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 29 мая 1026 г. в местности арльс, боучес-ду-рoн, провенсе-алпес-кoт д'азур, Франция в возрасте 78 лет и была похоронена в местности арльс, боучес-ду-рoн, провенсе-алпес-кoт д'азур, Франция.

Se casó con Raymond Count of Toulouse aproximadamente 0975. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
